text: I have been servicing generators for over 40 years. The Generator Inspection
I have created comes from customers who have been tagged for these Checklist
same items as well as conversations I have had with fire marshals, health
department officials, insurance adjusters, and Medicare/Medicaid staff.
(Most paperwork should go back 2 years. The 4-hour load tests required
every 3 years should have records going back past 2 tests, or 6 years.)

Also, remember you must transfer every transfer switch each month.

Calculate how long your fuel supply will last. Take fuel consumption
If your fuel supply is natural gas, you will need a letter from
your supplier that disaster will not interrupt service or keep a
minimum fuel supply onsite. Natural Gas Fuel Systems.
Have your policies available for generator malfunction—rental
availability, refueling, issues with residents needing life support.

Anything in any standard may be required at the discretion of the surveyor. The
items I have listed here are most common. If anything, else comes, up contact me
Your surveyors have hundreds of standards that they need to follow including
NFPA, ANSI, ASCE, ASSE, ASCE, ASI, ASTM, UL, and ICC. There is no way
anyone can be up to speed on all of these. However, This is why they all apply!
you hire an engineer. You pay the engineer to make sure you stay up to code.
